events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes venting trapped pressure to face level, which is a hazard (HAZ). This is a safety violation - trapped pressure should be vented safely away from personnel, not at face level. The work is in-progress and dangerous.

Decision: fail (dangerous/non-compliant)
Risk: critical (venting pressure at face level can cause injury)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: likely "installed-defective" since the manifold/vent arrangement is creating a hazard
Percent complete: estimate maybe 50
Value: 0
Confidence: high, say 0.9
Findings: trapped pressure being vented at face level - safety hazard
Actions: stop work, isolate/depressurize safely, redirect vent away from personnel, verify zero energy
Rationale: venting trapped pressure at face level exposes workers to potential injury; must follow mfr procedures for safe depressurization
References: mfr procedures
